description: A monochrome stone rubbing or ink drawing in the Chinese tradition depicting a seated Buddhist arhat (enlightened being). The figure is elderly and bald with exaggerated features — prominent brow ridges, deep-set eyes, and a strong jaw. He wears layered monastic robes rendered with fine cross-hatching patterns and sits on a rocky outcrop. His hands are pressed together in a gesture of meditation or prayer. Chinese calligraphic inscriptions fill the upper right portion of the composition. The overall style is that of a Tang or Song dynasty religious portrait.
